code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test13-MAY-2021Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating possible impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance and color, suggesting renal involvement, possibly nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy. However, low HbA1c argues against chronic hyperglycemia. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended. No evidence of urinary tract infection.
